title: Sub-Cubic Quantum Gate Synthesis via Stochastic Commutator Decomposition
abstract: We present Stochastic Commutator Synthesis, a hybrid quantum gate compilation framework that integrates Kuperberg's sub-cubic Solovay-Kitaev exponent c near 1.44042 with the error-tailoring machinery of randomized compilation. Classical Solovay-Kitaev implementations produce known word lengths and accumulate coherent approximation errors that degrade fault-tolerant threshold estimates. Kuperberg's 2023-2025 result reduces this via doubly exponential convergence and higher-order commutator decompositions. SCS augments this geometric backbone with a Gibbs-sampled stochastic choice of commutator factors at each recursion level, converting coherent synthesis residuals into incoherent, Pauli-twirl-compatible noise -- a property exploited by RC. Combined with RL-guided pre-synthesis, SCS achieves consistent T-count reductions of 10-25 percent and demonstrates fidelity gains of up to 35 percent on multi-fold Forrelation circuits on trapped-ion hardware such as Sandia QSCOUT. We situate SCS within the complexity-theoretic landscape established by the Raz-Tal oracle separation, arguing that low-error, noise-robust compilation of Forrelation-type circuits constitutes a practical pathway toward demonstrating this separation on physical hardware.
summary: This paper introduces Stochastic Commutator Synthesis, a new method for quantum gate compilation that combines efficient sub-cubic scaling with randomized compilation. By using Gibbs sampling to decompose commutators, it transforms harmful coherent errors into manageable incoherent noise, significantly improving circuit fidelity on trapped-ion hardware.
main_problem: The challenge of efficiently approximating arbitrary unitary operators using a discrete gate set while minimizing word length and preventing the accumulation of coherent approximation errors that degrade fault-tolerant thresholds.
main_result: The SCS framework achieves sub-cubic word-length scaling, reduces T-count by 10-25%, and demonstrates up to a 35% fidelity improvement on Forrelation circuits by converting coherent errors into incoherent noise.
method: A hybrid framework integrating Kuperberg's sub-cubic algorithm with Randomized Compilation (RC) using Gibbs-sampled commutator decomposition and RL-guided pre-synthesis (Q-PreSyn).
model_or_system: The framework is implemented and tested on the Sandia QSCOUT trapped-ion hardware using multi-fold Forrelation circuits and Clifford+T gate sets.
key_observables: Process fidelity, T-count, word-length exponent, trace distance, and operator/Frobenius norms.
important_parameters: Sub-cubic exponent c approx 1.44042, ensemble size K in [8, 32], temperature schedule with cooling factor beta in (0.5, 0.9), and precision epsilon between 2^-8 and 2^-12.
main_assumptions: Operates within the classical inverse-closed gate set setting.
figures_summary: The paper includes a comparison table of SCS, classical SKA, and GridSynth across various performance criteria.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of gate synthesis, presents the SCS framework (ensemble generation, Gibbs sampling, and recursive lifting), integrates it with RC and Q-PreSyn, demonstrates experimental results on Sandia QSCOUT, and situates the work within the complexity-theoretic context of the Raz-Tal oracle separation.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
